meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop drifting in tutorial hell. Learn how to implement a high-impact analytics setup using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate your SaaS MVP today.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a symptom of building without feedback loops. To escape, you must implement event-based analytics immediately. By integrating Antigravity for automated schema management and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you transform your MVP from a guessing game into a data-driven engine that tracks real user retention and revenue.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks in the trenches, stitching together boilerplate code and watching endless 'How-To' videos. Yet, your dashboard remains a graveyard of zero-data, and your product feels like a ghost ship. You are stuck in tutorial hell—the dangerous purgatory where you build features nobody wants because you lack the visibility to know what actually moves the needle.

True founders don't guess; they measure. The shift from a 'builder' to a 'founder' happens the moment you stop watching tutorials and start watching your user behavior. By leveraging Antigravity for automated infrastructure and Claude Code for surgical implementation, we are going to bypass the noise and establish a robust analytics pipeline in minutes, not days.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the single event that signifies user success. We define this schema using Antigravity to ensure consistent data typing across your entire stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable analytics schema for a SaaS MVP focusing on user onboarding completion, feature engagement, and subscription conversion events. Use TypeScript interfaces.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a tracking_schema.ts file based on the Antigravity generated schema.'

steps.title: Inject Analytics Middleware
steps.description: We need to capture events without bloating your business logic. We use Claude Code to wrap your service layer in a non-intrusive analytics interceptor.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a lightweight middleware pattern for event logging that captures user IDs and event timestamps without blocking the main thread.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Wrap existing API service methods with the new analytics interceptor middleware.'

steps.title: Automate Event Dispatchers
steps.description: Manual event tracking is prone to human error. We automate the dispatch mechanism so that every button click or form submit is tracked automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a centralized event dispatcher service that handles batching and error retries for analytics events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the centralized EventDispatcher class and integrate it into the project entry point.'

steps.title: Validate Data Integrity
steps.description: Garbage in, garbage out. We use Claude Code to verify that our analytics payloads match the schema defined in Step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a validation script that runs during integration tests to ensure all tracked events match the required schema properties.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Verify that all analytics event payloads conform to the tracking_schema.ts interfaces.'

steps.title: Real-time Dashboard Mapping
steps.description: Connect your event stream to a visualization tool to see your users in real-time. This is the death of tutorial hell.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a configuration manifest to map our internal event schema to standard analytics visualization tool formats.
steps.claude_code_command: claude deploy 'Push the analytics configuration to the production environment and verify stream connectivity.'

pro_tips: Never track everything. Focus exclusively on events that indicate a user has reached their 'Aha!' moment.
Use feature flags to toggle analytics logging, ensuring you don't overwhelm your database during high-traffic testing.
Anonymize PII (Personally Identifiable Information) at the edge before it ever hits your analytics database.
Implement 'heartbeat' events to track session duration, which is a leading indicator of churn.
Treat your analytics code with the same rigor as your payment processing code; bugs here cost you critical business insights.
content.next_steps: Once your data is flowing, spend 30 minutes daily reviewing your 'Drop-off' report. Identify where users stop interacting and use that data to refine your next feature sprint.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping products with real market feedback. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
